summary: Backend-only migration safety verification completed for startup retry idempotency and audited migration paths. All automated regression checks passed.

minor.issue: Manual runner executes SQL files without explicit per-file transaction (BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK), so partial apply risk remains if this script is used outside startup runner.

action_items: Align /app/apps/api/run-migration.js transaction behavior with src/scripts/runMigrations.ts for operational consistency.
critical_code_review_comments: Startup path is correctly transactional and rerun-safe; manual migration path still has partial-apply risk on failure because it is not transaction-wrapped per migration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Static regression checks now exist for trigger idempotency, index idempotency, top-level transaction statement conflicts, startup migration invocation, and transactional runner semantics. No runtime DB migration execution was possible due missing DATABASE_URL in this environment.
rca of the issue: Reported deployment failure mode is consistent with non-idempotent CREATE TRIGGER statements. Current audited SQL files now use DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS + CREATE TRIGGER in checked paths, which mitigates startup rerun crashes. Remaining risk is operational divergence if manual runner is used, since failures there may partially apply a migration file.
